extraction.one_line_summary: GROENEWEGEN Dylan won the Paris sprint after launching 400m out, sitting in KRISTOFF Alexander's slipstream before coming past.
tactical_patterns: ŠTYBAR Zdeněk attacked sub-5km on Champs-Élysées, showing QuickStep probes late sprint leadouts even without designated sprinter.
GROENEWEGEN Dylan deliberately positioned in KRISTOFF Alexander's draft before launch, using rival as windbreak rather than own leadout.
GUSEV Dmitriy survived solo 2km from 10km breakaway, suggesting peloton deliberately delayed chase to control sprint timing.

rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: positive: first Grand Tour stage win suggests readiness to challenge top-tier sprinters when positioned correctly
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: negative: launched first but lacked power to hold off drafted rival, tactical error or physical weakness
rider_signals.GREIPEL André: neutral: second place confirms continued speed but inability to position optimally without dominant leadout
rider_signals.IMPEY Daryl: neutral: instigated breakaway from first crossing suggests Orica-Scott prioritized visibility over sprint success
rider_signals.ŠTYBAR Zdeněk: neutral: late attack indicates QuickStep testing peloton fatigue thresholds even on ceremonial stage
